data.body:
Goal:
Make dispatch configuration cover every bundled workflow without turning setup into a giant flat list. Settings > Dispatch and `foolery setup` should organize bundled workflow targets into clear groups, and both surfaces should offer a fast "Add to all" path for seeding the same dispatch choice across eligible bundled workflow targets.

Verification:
1. Open Settings > Dispatch and verify every bundled workflow target is configurable, grouped into clear workflow-oriented sections, and not presented as one flat list.
2. Run `foolery setup`, enter the dispatch wizard, and verify the CLI presents the same bundled workflow coverage and grouped structure instead of a giant flat list.
3. In Settings > Dispatch, use "Add to all" to apply a dispatch choice across eligible bundled workflow targets, save, and confirm the expected settings are persisted while remaining individually editable.
4. Repeat the same bulk-apply flow through `foolery setup` and confirm it writes the same persisted configuration shape.

Constraints:
- Scope is limited to bundled workflows; custom workflow authoring is out of scope.
- UI and CLI organization should stay aligned so users do not learn two different dispatch models.
- "Add to all" should speed up setup without preventing per-target overrides afterward.
data.title: Improve bundled workflow dispatch setup
